label: SES-001 methodology-schemas CBM-content paper-test close-out (CBM engagement's first records): one BLOCKING finding (CR sub-domain hierarchy) flips Phase 1 readiness; three decisions DEC-001..003; one planning item PI-001; three decided_in references. Supersedes the SES-044-series authoring (commit d365ebc) which incorrectly continued CRMBUILDER's global sequence into a fresh engagement.

session.title: Methodology schemas — CBM content paper-test
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai paper-test conversation that produced methodology-schemas-cbm-paper-test-findings.md and the single decision flipping the CBM redo Phase 1 readiness call from 'ship as-is' to 'amend `domain` with `domain_parent_identifier` self-FK before Phase 1 opens.' Operating mode ARCHITECTURE per the project default; eight-element consequential decision template invoked twice (Finding 2 sub-domains, Finding 4 Cross-Domain Services). No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: contents of PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schemas-cbm-paper-test-kickoff.md (the conversation's seed). The Status: Ready-to-run header at the top of that file (added 05-18-26 17:30 after v0.5 and v0.6 shipped) was the operative pre-flight; the body's stale §Pre-flight numbered items (single-v2.db reference, v0.4-complete state) were skipped per the kickoff conversation's framing. The Status header's stale 'next SES likely SES-025' projection was first corrected at session-open against the CRMBUILDER snapshot state (SES-043 / DEC-107 / PI-017 highest applied at v0.6 closeout commit 3ffcd6c), then corrected again at close-out: CBM is a fresh engagement under the multi-tenant architecture; its records use a per-engagement counter starting at 001, so this paper-test's records are SES-001 / DEC-001..003 / PI-001 in CBM's per-engagement DB, not SES-044+ in any continued global sequence. The initial close-out authoring (commit d365ebc, superseded) used SES-044 / DEC-108..110 / PI-018; the dry-run review at SES-001 application time flagged the inconsistency and a follow-on commit regenerated the payload, apply prompt, and findings file with engagement-fresh identifiers.

CBM engagement gate cleared at session-open. The CBM engagement record existed in the meta DB and was the active engagement per the v0.5 slice D activation flow; per-engagement DB at crmbuilder-v2/data/engagements/{CBM}.db received this conversation's writes.

Reading pass before findings began: both repos' CLAUDE.md (crmbuilder + ClevelandBusinessMentoring); the four MVS schema specs at methodology-schema-specs/{domain,entity,process,crm_candidate}.md end-to-end; ui-PRD-v0.4.md §2 Out of Scope; the workstream plan §3 + §4 + §5; the spec guide §6 + §7; CBM Master PRD v2.6 (domains, personas, processes, cross-domain services); CBM Entity Inventory v1.6 (28 business concepts → 17 CRM entities); Domain PRDs for MN/MR/CR/FU; Sub-Domain Overviews for the four CR sub-domains; process .docx files MN×5 + MR×5 + CR×7 + FU×4; twelve Entity PRDs under PRDs/entities/; the Notes Service process doc. No methodology-extension/ folder exists in the CBM repo (the user-memory snapshot mentioning one was stale). FU has substantial content (Domain Overview + Domain PRD + four process docs) — the user-memory's 'not yet started' framing was also stale.

Domains tested: MN (Mentoring), MR (Mentor Recruitment), CR (Client Recruiting with four sub-domains Partner/Marketing/Events/Reactivation), FU (Fundraising). Pass 1 produced seven findings; Pass 2 produced one consolidated severity-ranking section across seven kickoff-named PIs plus five follow-on PIs.

Pass 1 findings — bucket distribution: CLEAN (5): F1 four-top-level-domains-map-to-`domain`, F5 surfaced-entity-names-map-via-`entity_scopes_to_domain`-many-to-many, F6 prioritized-backbone-maps-to-`process` (with one STRETCH on identifier-scheme), F7 CRM-candidate-set-accommodates-whatever-redo-produces. STRETCH (1): F6's mnemonic-identifier-legibility (MN-INTAKE vs PROC-NNN) — not blocking, PI-007 holds the v0.5+ enhancement. NO HOME (2): F3 personas-correctly-absent-because-evolved-Phase-1-doesn't-elicit-them (PI-003 v0.5+ work), F4 Cross-Domain-Services-correctly-unrepresented-because-services-are-infrastructure-not-mission-driven (PI-013 holds v0.5+ design question; Option A approved over Option C). BLOCKING (1): F2 CR-sub-domain-hierarchy — paper-test discovered the deferral assumption from `domain.md` §3.3.4 ('wait for real-engagement signal') was already met by CBM's existing content; Doug chose Option C (amend before Phase 1) over Option A' (defer with explicit PI tracking) after probing how the issue would be tracked under deferral.

Pass 2 severity ratings (1=v0.6+ nice-to-have, 3=Phase-2 friction, 5=on-runway blocking): PI-003 persona=4; PI-004 field/requirement/manual_config/test_spec=5 (biggest single v0.5+ workstream); PI-005 process_step growth=5 (joint with PI-004); PI-013 Cross-Domain Service=4; PI-014 Catalog FK=3 (dependency of PI-004); PI-015 methodology entity renderers=4; PI-016 router-level vocab=1. Follow-on PIs: PI-007 short_code=2, PI-009 entities-Domains-column=2, PI-010 entity variants/base-type=3 (joint with PI-004), PI-011 process scalar priority=2, PI-012 crm_candidate structured metadata=2.

Suggested v0.5+ workstream ordering after PI-001 lands: (1) PI-001 sub-domain amendment; (2) PI-004 + PI-014 + PI-010 joint workstream; (3) PI-005; (4) PI-003; (5) PI-013; (6) PI-015; (7) lower-priority follow-ons.

Final decision per kickoff rubric §6: amend N=1 schema before CBM redo Phase 1 starts. The schema is `domain`; the amendment is `domain_parent_identifier` self-FK following the topic.parent_topic pattern; the planning item is PI-001.
session.summary: Paper-test validation of the four MVS methodology entity schemas (`domain`, `entity`, `process`, `crm_candidate` — shipped in v0.4) against existing CBM domain content. Produced PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schemas-cbm-paper-test-findings.md (240 lines, seven Pass-1 findings + consolidated Pass-2 severity ratings + single closing decision). Pass 1 surfaced one BLOCKING finding (CR sub-domain hierarchy under flat v0.4 `domain`) that flipped the recommendation from 'ship Phase 1 on v0.4 as-is' to 'amend `domain` first.' Two methodology-correct NO HOME findings (personas — PI-003 v0.5+ work — and Cross-Domain Services — PI-013 v0.5+ design question) plus four CLEAN findings (top-level domains, surfaced entity names via many-to-many domain affiliation, Prioritized Backbone with one STRETCH on identifier-scheme legibility, CRM Candidate Set). Pass 2 produced the v0.5+ workstream-ordering signal.
session.artifacts_produced: One document committed to PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ on origin/main: methodology-schemas-cbm-paper-test-findings.md (240 lines, new). Plus this close-out payload close-out-payloads/ses_001.json and apply prompt prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-044.md. No CBM repo edits per kickoff's 'what this conversation does NOT do' constraints.
session.in_flight_at_end: PI-001 amendment workstream recommended as next conversation: a v0.4.1 (or first v0.5) planning conversation that produces the `domain_parent_identifier` self-FK amendment spec, slice plan, and CLAUDE-CODE prompts for the migration. The amendment is small — one nullable self-FK column, access-layer FK validation, list-endpoint hierarchy-aware ordering or parent filter, detail-pane render of parent and children — probably 2–3 slices. CBM redo Phase 1 waits on the amendment shipping; after it ships, `domain.md` §3.3.4 gets amended to reflect that sub-domain hierarchy is in scope, then the first CBM redo Phase 1 conversation opens against the amended schema. The two Pass-2 high-severity PIs that should open after PI-001 (PI-004+014+010 joint workstream for entity richness; PI-005 for process_step growth) remain queued but not yet sequenced against CBM redo's Phase 1 vs Phase 3 timeline.

decisions.title: Paper-test single decision: amend `domain` with `domain_parent_identifier` self-FK for sub-domain hierarchy before CBM redo Phase 1 opens
decisions.context: The methodology-schemas CBM-content paper-test (the kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schemas-cbm-paper-test-kickoff.md) tested the four v0.4 MVS methodology entity schemas against existing CBM domain content. The kickoff's decision rubric §6 requires a single closing decision: either 'ship CBM redo Phase 1 on v0.4 as-is' or 'amend N schemas before Phase 1 starts, in this order.' Pass 1 surfaced one BLOCKING finding (Finding 2 — CR's four-sub-domain structure under flat v0.4 `domain`). The `domain.md` schema spec §3.3.4 had anticipated this concern by deferring self-referential parent-child hierarchy with the rationale 'wait for real-engagement signal'; the paper-test discovered the signal was already present in CBM's existing Master PRD §3.5 + four CR Sub-Domain Overview documents + seven sub-domain processes. The minimum-viable principle that justifies deferral does not apply when the design conversation lacked the inputs to recognize a requirement that the paper-test makes explicit.
decisions.decision: **Amend `domain` with a nullable `domain_parent_identifier` self-FK before CBM redo Phase 1 opens.** The amendment follows the existing `topic.parent_topic` pattern: nullable TEXT column matching `^DOM-\d{3}$` when set, validated at the access layer to refer to a live `domain` record and not to the row's own identifier (no self-loop). List endpoint supports either hierarchy-aware ordering or a `?parent=DOM-NNN` filter (build-planning decides which); detail pane renders parent and children; create/edit dialogs include a Parent combo box backed by live `domain` records. The amendment opens as the first v0.5+ planning conversation (or as v0.4.1 — naming TBD at the planning conversation's open). CBM redo Phase 1 waits on the amendment shipping; after it ships, `domain.md` §3.3.4 gets amended to reflect that sub-domain hierarchy is in scope, then the first CBM redo Phase 1 conversation opens against the amended schema. Tracked as PI-001.
decisions.rationale: Three considerations. **First**, CBM's existing Master PRD §3.5 explicitly declares CR as a single domain 'organized into four sub-domains — Partner Relationship Management, Outreach and Marketing, Workshops and Event Management, and Client Reactivation and Recovery.' Each sub-domain has its own coordinator, its own process inventory (7 sub-domain-level processes), and its own Sub-Domain Overview document. The CR parent provides cross-sub-domain reporting, comparative channel effectiveness analysis, and shared audience strategy. This is real organizational structure, not documentation artifact. **Second**, proceeding flat under Option A (CR as one `domain`, sub-domains as prose) or Option A' (Option A with explicit high-priority PI opened) defers a known requirement rather than a speculative one — the deferral case rests on 'we'll see what real use produces' but the real use signal is already in front of us. **Third**, the amendment is small (nullable self-FK, minor endpoint and UI work — probably 2–3 slices) and proceeding flat then back-filling parent FKs after Phase 1 has authored records is more friction than amending first.
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option A — CR as one flat `domain` record.** Sub-domain structure as prose in `domain_description`; all seven CR-area processes attach via `process_domain_identifier=DOM-003`; Sub-Domain Overview documents have no schema home. Rejected: structurally simplifies CBM's existing organizational model and forces lopsided process scannability on the Processes panel. **Option A' — Option A with explicit high-priority PI opened.** Adds PI-NNN tracking with trigger pre-captured and v0.5+ priority ordering. Rejected after Doug probed how the issue would be tracked under deferral: pre-fired-trigger language acknowledges the deferral case is weak; if the signal is already present, the methodologically honest move is to amend, not to track. **Option B — Four flat sub-domain records with no CR parent.** Domain Inventory shows 7 records; processes attach to four different sub-domain FKs; cross-sub-domain reporting and shared audience strategy can't be expressed. Rejected: loses CR-as-parent business concept that CBM operates against.
decisions.consequences: PI-001 opens as the first v0.5+ planning conversation. The amendment workstream is small (likely 2–3 slices: schema migration + access layer + UI dialogs / detail pane). After it ships, `domain.md` §3.3.4 is amended to reflect that sub-domain hierarchy is in scope. CBM redo Phase 1 opens against the amended schema with the Domain Inventory expected to contain ~8 records: MN, MR, CR (parent), CR-Partner, CR-Marketing, CR-Events, CR-Reactivate, FU — with the four CR-children pointing to CR via `domain_parent_identifier`. The methodology spec for `domain` accepts the new field as additive (existing flat records have nullable parent = null, no behavior change). The high-severity Pass-2 PIs (PI-004+014+010 joint, PI-005, PI-003, PI-013, PI-015) remain queued for v0.5+ as documented in the findings file §3.3.

decisions.title: Cross-Domain Services unrepresented in v0.4 Phase 1; CDS-owned entities handled by `entity_scopes_to_domain`; PI-013 retains the unresolved v0.5+ design question
decisions.context: CBM's Master PRD §4 declares four Cross-Domain Services structurally parallel to domains: MST-SVC-001 Notes Service (all four domains, owns Note entity); MST-SVC-002 Email Service (all four domains, no entities); MST-SVC-003 Calendar Service (all four domains, no entities); MST-SVC-004 Survey Service (MN and CR, owns Survey and Survey Response). At least one service-internal process document exists (PRDs/services/NOTES/NOTES-MANAGE.docx). The v0.4 schema has no `service` entity type and no equivalent. PI-013 explicitly tracks the unresolved design question — is CDS a separate methodology entity type, subsumed into `process` once `process_kind` lands in v0.5+, or dropped entirely. The paper-test §Pass-1 special-checks asked: does v0.4 need a representation, or can CBM redo Phase 1 produce usable output without CDS data?
decisions.decision: **Option A — services not represented as v0.4 records; CDS-owned entities via `entity_scopes_to_domain`; PI-013 retains the v0.5+ design question.** The redo's Phase 1 Domain Inventory does not contain `domain` records for Notes / Email / Calendar / Survey. CDS-owned entities (Note, Survey, Survey Response) become `entity` records with appropriate many-to-many domain scoping: Note scopes to MN/MR/CR/FU; Survey and Survey Response scope to MN/CR. Email and Calendar own no entities, so nothing to record. Service-internal processes like NOTES-MANAGE are not surfaced as Phase 1 `process` records — they are treated as platform infrastructure not subject to Phase 1's mission-driven Prioritized Backbone inventory.
decisions.rationale: The evolved methodology's Phase 1 elicits 'the big areas of work the client's mission forces the organization to address.' Notes / Email / Calendar / Surveys are platform infrastructure that supports mission-driven work; they don't pass the priority test ('if Notes Service stopped tomorrow, the mission would still continue'). Surfacing them as `domain` records would distort the mission-criticality framing of the Domain Inventory and effectively redefine 'domain' to include infrastructure, which would propagate confusion to every downstream engagement. v0.4's existing `entity_scopes_to_domain` many-to-many mechanism handles CDS-owned entities correctly without interpretive stretch — Note scopes to all four domains, Survey scopes to two, and the schema doesn't need to know they're 'cross-domain service entities.' The deferral fits the minimum-viable principle properly here (unlike Finding 2 sub-domains, where the signal was already present): CDS friction has not yet surfaced in any real engagement; PI-013's design question remains genuinely open.
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option B — Record services as `domain` records with a convention.** Domain Inventory contains additional flat records like `DOM-005 — Notes Service` with `domain_description` flagging infrastructure framing. Service-internal processes attach via `process_domain_identifier` to their service-as-domain record. Rejected: distorts the mission-criticality framing of `domain`; downstream renderers and consultants must learn the implicit convention 'some domains aren't really domains.' **Option C — BLOCKING. Add a `service` entity type before CBM redo Phase 1 opens.** Larger amendment than Finding 2's self-FK — new table, new vocabulary, new sidebar entry, new acceptance criteria — probably a full v0.4.2 workstream. Rejected: PI-013's design question is genuinely unresolved (separate type vs `process_kind` extension vs drop), unlike Finding 2's sub-domain question which had a clear amendment path; making a structural commitment now risks shipping the wrong shape.
decisions.consequences: v0.4 ships unchanged for CDS handling. Note, Survey, and Survey Response become entity records with multi-domain `entity_scopes_to_domain` references in CBM redo's Phase 1. Service-internal process documents (NOTES-MANAGE and any future ones) are not authored as Phase 1 `process` records; if their content is wanted later, it lives in Phase 3 process docs with appropriate domain attribution or in consultant-side context. PI-013 remains in the high-severity tier of the v0.5+ queue (severity 4) and will need resolution before Phase 3 work hits service-internal processes. The CBM redo's Phase 3 work surfaces the actual friction (if any) that drives PI-013's eventual design conversation.

decisions.title: Pass 2 v0.5+ workstream ordering signal from the paper-test
decisions.context: The paper-test's Pass 2 produced severity ratings across the seven deferred PIs the kickoff names (PI-003 / 004 / 005 / 013 / 014 / 015 / 016) plus five follow-on PIs surfaced across Findings 1–7 (PI-007 / 009 / 010 / 011 / 012). The kickoff explicitly names Pass 2 as 'advisory; produces the prioritization signal for which v0.5+ PI to open first.' The paper-test is the right moment to capture that signal as a decision rather than only as findings-file text — the signal is consumed by future planning conversations that decide which workstreams to open and in what order.
decisions.decision: **Capture the paper-test's Pass 2 prioritization signal as the recommended v0.5+ workstream ordering after PI-001 lands:** (1) PI-001 sub-domain hierarchy amendment — BLOCKING for CBM redo Phase 1 per DEC-001. (2) PI-004 + PI-014 + PI-010 joint workstream — entity richness (fields + requirements + manual_config + test_spec + catalog FK + variants). Likely the biggest single workstream of v0.5+ and a prerequisite for CBM redo Phase 3. (3) PI-005 — process_step growth; joint candidate with PI-004 since both unblock Phase 3 process work. (4) PI-003 — `persona` entity type; CBM Phase 3 process docs depend on persona references. (5) PI-013 — Cross-Domain Service design question (resolve before Phase 3 surfaces service-internal processes; severity 4). (6) PI-015 — methodology entity renderers; pulls hard once Phase 1 content exists and stakeholders ask for Domain Inventory documents. (7) Lower-priority follow-ons: PI-007, PI-009, PI-011, PI-012, PI-016 as quality-of-life or speculative. This ordering is a recommendation; the actual workstream-opening cadence remains under Doug's control per conversation.
decisions.rationale: Three principles guided the ordering. **First**, BLOCKING items go first (PI-001). **Second**, items that unblock the next methodology phase have priority over items that unblock later phases; PI-004 and PI-005 unblock CBM Phase 3 (the largest remaining body of methodology work), where PI-013 and PI-015 unblock Phase 3 friction but at a smaller scale and where PI-016 is near-theoretical until external clients exist. **Third**, joint workstreams that share design decisions land together — PI-004 / PI-014 / PI-010 all touch the same field-and-catalog-and-variant design space and are cheaper to design together than separately. The severity ratings in the findings file §3.1 / §3.2 provide the quantitative backing; this decision captures the ordering as a single record so future planning conversations have a citable signal.
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Strict severity ordering (PI-004=5 → PI-005=5 → PI-003=4 → PI-013=4 → PI-015=4 → ...).** Rejected because severity alone doesn't capture phase-coupling (joint workstreams) or PI-001's BLOCKING status. **Open every PI in parallel.** Rejected because v0.5+ planning conversations are sequential and each workstream consumes design attention; the ordering is the question the paper-test should answer. **No ordering captured — let each planning conversation pick.** Rejected because the signal would be lost; the paper-test is the right moment to record it.
decisions.consequences: The recommended ordering is documented in this decision and in the findings file §3.3. Future planning conversations cite this decision when justifying which workstream they open. The ordering is non-binding — Doug may sequence workstreams differently based on CBM redo's actual Phase 1 surfaces, parallel v1 application work, or other context this paper-test couldn't anticipate. PI-001 is the only ordering element that is non-negotiable (per DEC-001's BLOCKING categorization).

planning_items.title: `domain_parent_identifier` self-FK on `domain` for sub-domain hierarchy
planning_items.description:
Surfaced by the methodology-schemas CBM-content paper-test (SES-001) as the single BLOCKING finding (Finding 2). CBM's Master PRD §3.5 declares Client Recruiting (CR) as a single domain 'organized into four sub-domains — Partner Relationship Management, Outreach and Marketing, Workshops and Event Management, and Client Reactivation and Recovery.' Each sub-domain has its own coordinator, its own process inventory (7 sub-domain-level processes across the four), its own Sub-Domain Overview document. The CR parent provides cross-sub-domain reporting, comparative channel effectiveness analysis, and shared audience strategy. v0.4's `domain` schema is flat; `domain.md` §3.3.4 explicitly defers self-referential parent-child hierarchy pending 'real-engagement signal' from the redo's Phase 1. The paper-test discovered the signal is already in front of us — the deferral assumption depends on inputs the original schema-design conversation didn't have. DEC-001 settles this PI as the first v0.5+ planning conversation, opening before CBM redo Phase 1.

Scope of the amendment: a nullable TEXT column `domain_parent_identifier` matching `^DOM-\d{3}$` when set; access-layer validation that the value refers to a live `domain` record and is not the row's own identifier (no self-loop); the existing soft-delete / restore semantics extend trivially to the parent FK (soft-deleting a parent does not cascade to children; the references-table soft-delete pattern doesn't apply because this is a direct FK column, not a references-entity edge). List endpoint either gains hierarchy-aware default ordering or a `?parent=DOM-NNN` filter — the planning conversation decides which. Detail pane renders the parent identifier (read-only label or click-through) and an inline children list (via list endpoint with parent filter). Create and edit dialogs add a Parent combo box backed by live `domain` records, sorted by identifier ascending. Identifier-format validation on POST/PUT/PATCH including reject of self-FK assignment. Alembic migration adding the column nullable (no default — existing flat records get null, no behavior change). Acceptance criteria follow the `domain.md` §3.7 pattern: schema migration applies cleanly forward and backward; FK validation rejects bad references; CRUD round-trips; CBM-redo sample records (8 records covering MN, MR, CR-parent, CR-Partner, CR-Marketing, CR-Events, CR-Reactivate, FU) author and re-fetch correctly.

Probable workstream shape: 2–3 slices. Slice A — schema migration + access layer + API endpoint updates + tests. Slice B — UI (Parent combo, detail-pane parent render, children list, master-pane hierarchy treatment). Optional slice C — `domain.md` schema spec amendment and any README / status entity touches. The naming (v0.4.1 vs absorb into v0.5's first slice) is the first decision the planning conversation makes. After the amendment ships and the schema spec is updated, the CBM redo Phase 1 first conversation opens against the amended schema. PI-001 closes when the amendment ships and the schema spec amendment is committed.
